steps.label: cause
steps.text: Bordeaux varieties differ in budding, ripening, structure and performance under a given season's weather.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Blending lots and varieties lets the producer combine components that performed differently and balance fruit, acidity, tannin and body.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The final wine can be more balanced and stylistically consistent than a wine dependent on one underperforming component.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: blending varieties with different seasonal responses helps manage vintage variation and balance the final wine.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
